title: Availability-first hardening: schedule ≠ slot, _staffAvailWithTime covers both intents, greeting uses real clock
failing_behavior:
Three related regressions:
  (1) AI said 'Tracy is available from 9:00 AM today' (from shift start time in schedule data), then when booking was attempted at 9AM said 'Sorry, 9AM is already booked' — direct contradiction caused by treating schedule start time as proof of slot availability.
  (2) _staffAvailWithTime only guarded staff_availability intent, leaving a gap: booking_request + named staff + time + date + no services bypassed all slot validation.
  (3) AI greeted customers with 'Good day' at 9 PM — does not reflect actual time of day.
root_cause:
Three root causes:
  (1) Prompt lacked an explicit rule forbidding schedule/start-time as proof of slot availability.
  (2) _staffAvailWithTime condition only matched intent === 'staff_availability', missing the booking_request + no-services gap that earlyCheckReady also misses (earlyCheckReady requires services.length > 0).
  (3) _buildPrompt() injected current time via nowStr but had no explicit greeting rule — Claude defaulted to 'Good day' as a generic greeting.
fix_summary:
Four-part fix in nailsalon/receptionist.js:
  (1) Prompt: CRITICAL — SCHEDULE ≠ SLOT AVAILABILITY rule added. Explicitly forbids treating shift start time as proof of slot availability. 'When a customer asks about a SPECIFIC TIME, classify as booking_request and let the system validate the slot.'
  (2) Prompt: intent classification updated — staff_availability = GENERAL schedule only (no specific time). 'If customer names a SPECIFIC TIME, classify as booking_request, NOT staff_availability.'
  (3) Code: _staffAvailWithTime condition extended to cover both intents: staff_availability OR (booking_request AND services.length === 0). Closes the earlyCheckReady gap.
  (4) Prompt: GREETING RULE section added using _timeOfDay variable (morning/afternoon/evening). Explicitly forbids 'Good day'.

conversation.content:
[CORRECT] Let me check that slot for you — what service were you thinking?
[STATE:{"intent":"booking_request","services":[],"staff":"Tracy","date":"2026-04-19","time":"09:00","name":null,"phone":null,"lang":"en","pendingAction":null,"existingBookingId":null}]

[CORRECT] Yes, Tracy works Fridays from 9 AM to 7 PM! What time works for you?
[STATE:{"intent":"staff_availability","services":[],"staff":"Tracy","date":"2026-04-17","time":null,"name":null,"phone":null,"lang":"en","pendingAction":"booking_offer","existingBookingId":null}]

bad_examples.label: BANNED — schedule as availability proof
bad_examples.text: Tracy starts at 9 AM today, so yes she's available at 9 AM!

bad_examples.label: BANNED — schedule as availability proof
bad_examples.text: Tracy works from 9 AM, so you can book her at 9 AM.

bad_examples.label: BANNED — wrong greeting at evening
bad_examples.text: Good day! How can I help you today?

bad_examples.label: BANNED — wrong classification for specific time

good_examples.label: CORRECT — specific time classified as booking_request
CORRECT — general schedule = staff_availability, no time
CORRECT — morning greeting
good_examples.text: Good morning! How can I help you today?

good_examples.label: CORRECT — evening greeting
good_examples.text: Good evening! What can I do for you?

verify_fix_string_2: NEVER use "Good day" — it does not reflect the actual time of day.
notes: The _staffAvailWithTime guard now covers two paths: (1) staff_availability + time + named staff + date — the original fix. (2) booking_request + time + named staff + date + no services — the gap where earlyCheckReady misses (earlyCheckReady requires services.length > 0). Together with earlyCheckReady (booking_request + services + staff + date + time), all three paths that could produce schedule-vs-slot contradictions are now intercepted and validated against Firestore before Claude's response is shown.
